So what that looks like and just kind of the workflow is not established.

of what I want to

mean, it's, yeah, there's a lot of like people involved internally on your end and, you know, there's a lot of moving parts and we've, you know, typically I could tell you what this works usually with like most of our clients and granted, like we are very used to, at least the clients that I've worked with so far, we do have some, you know, enterprise clients, but we also work with a lot of startups, a lot of small teams.

With Scrunch, I know that we don't have visibility.

From the SEMrush, we have our version of like Ahrefs and I think SEMrush, quasi.

But once we have how the teams, who the right people are for, for you guys.

And for us, we'll then, I think, be able to know exactly what tooling we're using for what type of things, and then we're measuring all of our priorities against real tangible expected outcomes so that we can communicate that up to our CMO, who on a weekly basis is kind of getting these reports on how did we do last week, and if those questions come up of, which this is what I'm noticing over the last couple of weeks, is we'll have the data and then the question comes and it's like, what, how are we thinking about this?
Where are we prioritizing this?
What are we doing about this?

Those are kind of the things I'd love to just avoid so that, again, it all comes back down to how do we have fun in the creative space to do the work that we can and we can't have.
On our creative space, if we're just constantly reacting to requests that we're getting and stuff.
So just basically, how do we make work as easy as possible?
And I think we'll have a good idea of what that looks like pretty soon.
Okay.
Yeah.
Thank you so much for that context.
That's really helpful.
Just to, okay.

And then we can like build some projections for these refreshes.
So that's easier for you to like build reporting and like, you know, answer these types of questions internally.
So I think that that will be helpful for you.
I know that last week you were talking to Joe about like the net new content strategy.
And it was suggested that Kezia takes a look at these topic clusters and see if they make sense.
So there are links in the agenda.
Feel free to leave comments.
This is all very, like, brand new.

And we would, you know, when we build it out, we do need to, like, sync and see, like, avoid duplicate work and also, like, see where we want to play and where we think we can win, depending on what the broader strategy is.

And I can, I'm pretty sure that it's going to be going to really, like, targeting very more niche queries, kind of lower the funnel, also using Scrunch to see where we're not, where you're not performing well, like, what kind of queries you're not being surfaced for or your competitors are being surfaced for first.
So we can also guide, like, assignment generation based on the Scrunch insights.
So I think that would be also another action item from this meeting as well.
Yeah, perfect.
And I think really helpful for me to know and just get some context, especially since being out.
Have you, in your audit, was it looking at all of the pages and kind of has

Has been any kind of mapping exercise to say like, okay, this is what these terms would typically point to and kind of have that like full view or is it kind of, okay, we're zooming in, we're looking at whether the opportunity is based off the click decline and then only kind of mapping for those just to see what information you already have as well when I'm making these suggestions and comments.
Yeah, yeah.
So the immediate focus was on just the click decline.
However, what we did was Festus and you, you shared the spreadsheets with all of the content that was published in different years.
And one of the things we did was we merged all of that content together.

Like publish dates, like buyer's journey, persona, like audience segment, like all of those were pulled from those spreadsheets.

So that was another goal was just like unifying all of that information in one place because it, you know, from our conversations with Abhi early on, you know, she really wanted to have like a unified content view.
So we brought all of that together at Airtable.
So that was like, those were like our two guiding principles.
Like let's understand what's losing traffic right now, what we need to tackle, because also LLMs kind of, LLMs prefer fresh content.
Like they are focused very much on the content from like the past six months and anything that's not being refreshed and it's losing clicks is also a great material for LLM optimization.
So that's what we focused on and also like unifying all of these separate spreadsheets.
Okay, perfect.
No problem.
I'll, I had a quick, quick look into the doc, but I will go through and do a little bit more of a thorough review.
And leave any comments and we can kind of follow up the conversation from there.
Yeah, yeah, perfect.
Festus, I will reach out in Slack just to see what kind of pieces you're optimizing next week, so we don't do duplicate work.
I know that Jo reached out over email last week, so we're good for this week, I think.
A quick note on that one as well.

That might change, but I will follow back with that one.

Keywords that we would have been talking, or any new blog listeners, so she should be able to do that.
Okay, okay, sounds good.
Any last-minute questions, comments?
Just, you know, go ahead, Jim.
I was just going to say we're happy to connect about the Optimizely thing, and we'll just set up a very easy workflow for you guys in there.
It's going to be a no-brainer for you to learn it.
So we'll make it as user-friendly as possible, but feel free to reach out to me on the side.
And probably best if we set it up with, like, one email address, like we did with the Sanity Access, so.
Yeah, yeah, we could use the T-mail Access for that.
I'll sync with Andy right after this call and get back to you on that.
Like I said, I don't see a reason why we shouldn't be able to use it.
It'll be easier for everyone.
Okay, I have to hop as well, too, so.
Yeah, yeah.
All right, well, thanks, guys.
I'll follow up in Slack later.
Thanks.
Okay, perfect.
Thanks a Bye-bye.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss content strategy, workflow optimization, and alignment between Diligent and growthx.ai teams.](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Content refresh efforts progressing: 5 published, 5 more coming soon; scaling to 8/week by Aug 25](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=6.0)
  - [Need to establish clear workflow and tooling (e.g., Optimizely) for seamless collaboration](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0)
  - [Addressing 600+ broken internal links; prioritizing 404 fixes first](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [Aligning on CRO web strategy and how growthx.ai can best support Diligent's goals](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)

## Topics

### Content Refresh Progress

  - [1 refresh published, 5 more coming soon](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [Scaling to 8 refreshes/week by Aug 25](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=180.0)
  - [SEO testing group and Looker Studio tracking to be set up](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [44 refresh opportunities in pipeline, 30-40 more to review](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=558.0)
  - [Focusing on blogs that lost \>50 clicks in last 3 months](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=572.0)

### Workflow Optimization

  - [Moving work to Optimizely platform for improved tracking and review process](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0)
  - [John to set up user-friendly workflow in Optimizely](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=262.0)
  - [Festus now has Slack access; can be tagged for content updates](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=341.0)
  - [Need to establish clear SLAs and team responsibilities](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=976.0)

### Broken Links and 404s

  - [600+ internal broken links identified](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=376.0)
  - [Prioritizing fixing 404s first, then re-evaluating remaining broken links](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=400.0)
  - [Discussion on streamlining 404 fix process in Sanity CMS](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=517.0)

### CRO Web Strategy Alignment

  - [Tyler seeking clarity on how growthx.ai can support Diligent's CRO strategy](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)
  - [Need for congruency between content efforts and engagement-driving initiatives](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=939.0)
  - [Importance of measuring expected outcomes for prioritization and business case building](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=696.0)

### Communication and Visibility

  - [Emphasizing need for direct, frequent communication (e.g., via Slack)](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=1192.0)
  - [Discussing potential internalization of tools like Scrunch for better visibility](https://fathom.video/share/RyH8STMmdVTDURAAWsq-NJXRzKjay2hH?tab=summary&timestamp=1282.0)
